text.title: Controller, DTO Records, and MapStruct Mapper
text.summary: REST controller with Bean Validation, Java record DTOs, and MapStruct mapper setup.

rule.statement: Controllers must never return entities. Always map to a DTO record before returning. Use @Valid on @RequestBody. Prefer @PageableDefault over manual page parameter parsing.
rule.rationale: Returning entities exposes lazy-loaded proxy objects to Jackson serialisation, which can trigger LazyInitializationException after the transaction ends or expose internal fields.

rule.statement: Use Java 17+ records for DTOs. Records are immutable and compact. Use jakarta.validation constraints on request records.
rule.rationale: Records eliminate boilerplate (constructors, equals, hashCode, toString). Immutability prevents unintended mutation of request data as it flows through service layers.

rule.statement: Use MapStruct with componentModel = SPRING. Ensure annotation processing is wired in pom.xml or build.gradle — MapStruct does not work without it and fails silently at runtime.
rule.rationale: Missing annotation processor configuration causes MapStruct to generate no implementation. The bean is missing at startup; agents miss this because the compiler produces no error.
